project.name: zca-desktop
project.summary: Cross-platform Zalo desktop client (Tauri v2 + Rust) built on the zca-rust unofficial API. Supports multiple accounts logged in simultaneously and coexistence with the user's other Zalo devices. agent-harness-kit is the dev control center (model routing + feature tracking + review gates).

in: Multi-account SessionManager + account switcher + per-account routing

risks.title: Unofficial API / ToS + account-ban risk
risks.mitigation: Treat as personal-use tool; rate-limit; surface warnings; no automation that looks like spam.

risks.title: Cookie/session expiry invalidates login
risks.mitigation: Detect 401/expiry, mark account re-auth-needed, prompt re-import.

risks.title: Multi-device same-account conflicts (imei/session)
risks.mitigation: Each account = distinct exported session; verify coexistence (device-coexistence feature) before claiming support.

risks.title: Secret handling (cookies are bearer tokens)
risks.mitigation: OS keychain via keyring; never log/echo cookie values; gitignore cred files.

risks.title: zca-rust endpoints only 43/149 verified live
risks.mitigation: Build on verified endpoints first; gate unverified behind live-test evidence.

decisions.title: Tauri v2 + Rust core hosts zca-rust sessions; webview = UI
zca-rust consumed as git dependency (not crates.io)
Multi-account = many in-process API+Listener sessions in a SessionManager
Credentials imported as ZaloDataExtractor JSON; stored in OS keychain
Cross-install sync relay is out of MVP (stretch, ADR-gated)
zca-rust consumed as a pinned git rev (08698e1c), rustls TLS — no native bindings, no new ADR
